name: writing-plans pre-emit existence checks (PR-1 of v2.5.0)
symptom: writing-plans produced Phase 5 Lane B tasks that imported @tanstack/react-query and mounted QueryClientProvider without verifying package.json. Verified at Builder dispatch time: @tanstack/react-query was NOT in deps on the target branch. Builder correctly returned NEEDS_CONTEXT; without Builder discipline, an under-disciplined agent could have silently pnpm-add'd the dep + preempted the planned phase-perf adoption pattern.
schema_note: Each test_cases entry has: category (deps-present | deps-absent | adoption-plan), prompt (realistic plan-authoring directive), expected_behavior (what the post-fix writing-plans should do). This is a behavioral eval — run via skill-creator's eval workflow (writing-plans dispatched with each prompt; check the produced plan against expected_behavior).

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task to add a Zod schema for a new user-preferences form. Backend is Next.js App Router Server Actions in src/actions/. zod@3 is in package.json devDependencies.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Emit task referencing zod without escalation. Optionally cite the package.json verification in the task body.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task to call supabase.rpc('get_visible_work_items', { p_user_id }). The function CREATE FUNCTION get_visible_work_items is in supabase/migrations/044_visible_items.sql.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Emit task referencing the RPC without escalation. Verification: grep CREATE FUNCTION get_visible_work_items in migrations matches.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task to read from the work_items table for the dashboard. CREATE TABLE work_items is in supabase/migrations/001_init.sql with columns id, org_id, title, status, created_at.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Emit task referencing work_items table without escalation. Existence check only — don't claim to have verified column types or RLS shape (that's Builder's job).

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task using pgmq for a background-job queue. supabase/migrations/180_pgmq.sql creates the pgmq extension and pgmq.read / pgmq.send RPCs.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Emit task. Don't verify call-signature shape of pgmq.read at plan time (Builder catches the .schema('pgmq').rpc(...) vs .rpc('pgmq_read') mechanical bug per STRENGTH-2).

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task adding unit tests via node:test runner. package.json scripts have "test": "node --test --import=tsx tests/**/*.test.ts".
test_cases.expected_behavior: Emit task. Existence of node:test runner is verified; test-runner shape (whether the import flag handles dynamic imports correctly) remains Builder's discovery.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task that imports @tanstack/react-query and mounts QueryClientProvider at app root. Project hasn't adopted TanStack Query yet — no entry in package.json, no provider mounted anywhere.
test_cases.expected_behavior: ESCALATE as Open Question. Plan must not silently emit a task that requires a missing cross-cutting dep. The escalation should name the dep + ask CTO for the install path.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task to call rpc('upsert_user_notification_pref', { p_user_id, p_pref }). The RPC does not exist in any migration in supabase/migrations/.
test_cases.expected_behavior: ESCALATE as Open Question. The RPC name is referenced in spec but no migration creates it — needs a migration task (DBE work) before any caller task.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task that reads from the audit_events_v2 table for the new compliance dashboard. No CREATE TABLE audit_events_v2 exists; the current audit table is audit_events.
test_cases.expected_behavior: ESCALATE as Open Question. Either the spec means audit_events (typo) or it intends a new audit_events_v2 (requires migration). Producer must not pick silently.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task using vitest's vi.mock() to stub the Supabase client in unit tests. Project uses node:test exclusively; vitest is NOT in package.json or any test config.
test_cases.expected_behavior: ESCALATE as Open Question. Two valid paths: (a) re-tool tasks to use node:test's mock module, or (b) adopt vitest (cross-cutting decision). Producer surfaces both, doesn't pick.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task that imports @sentry/nextjs SDK and wraps the global error handler. Not in package.json; no Sentry integration exists.
test_cases.expected_behavior: ESCALATE as Open Question. Sentry adoption is cross-cutting (DSN config, source-map upload, env vars). Producer must not silently add the dep.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task that adds PostHog event tracking on the dashboard. PostHog is not in package.json BUT docs/plans/phase-perf-analytics.md describes the planned PostHog adoption with the provider mount point + privacy-mode config + env-var naming.
test_cases.expected_behavior: HANDSHAKE with CTO. Surface: 'PostHog absent from package.json; adoption plan exists at docs/plans/phase-perf-analytics.md. Options: (a) merge this plan's PostHog usage with the adoption plan's wiring, (b) defer the analytics tasks until the adoption plan ships, (c) accept duplication with documented rationale.' Don't silently pnpm add.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task that uses Drizzle ORM for new query patterns. Drizzle is not in package.json BUT docs/audits/orm-migration-2026-05.md proposes Drizzle adoption with schema layout + migration strategy.
test_cases.expected_behavior: HANDSHAKE with CTO. Adoption-plan handshake: surface the conflict with the ORM-migration audit doc; don't preempt the audit's decision tree.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task that calls supabase.realtime.channel().on('presence', ...) for the chat-presence indicator. The realtime-presence pattern is documented in docs/plans/realtime-presence.md but not yet wired in src/.
test_cases.expected_behavior: HANDSHAKE with CTO. Surface: pattern documented but not yet implemented; this task would be the first implementation of the documented design. Either co-implement with the realtime-presence plan or defer.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task that uses zod schemas for API contracts. Zod IS in package.json — but docs/plans/api-validation-strategy.md specifies zod-to-openapi as the canonical wiring with a specific schema-export pattern.
test_cases.expected_behavior: HANDSHAKE with CTO even though zod existence check passes. The adoption plan constrains HOW zod is used; producer must align with the plan's wiring rather than authoring an ad-hoc schema-import pattern.

test_cases.prompt: Plan a task that uses next/dynamic with ssr: false to lazy-load a chart component. Next.js is in package.json BUT docs/plans/server-actions-migration.md is specifically removing client-only dynamic imports in favor of Server Actions + RSC.
test_cases.expected_behavior: HANDSHAKE with CTO. Adoption-plan handshake surfaces the conflict: this task moves the codebase in the opposite direction of the server-actions migration. Producer must not silently emit the regression.
